evals.name: explicit-interview-me-trigger
evals.prompt: Interview me before you build this — I want the dashboard redesign spec locked down first.
evals.expected_output: Triggers intent-lock. Phase 0 runs silently (cold read, prediction v0, divergent readings, admission-threshold defaults) with nothing but the eventual option-picker reaching the user. No GOAL UNIFIED marker, no convergence-gate text, no per-round price is printed. Round 1 opens with a single option-picker call (max 3 hypotheses + escape) whose preamble states the open question verbatim.

evals.name: lock-the-goal-trigger
evals.prompt: Lock the goal before we start: I need a migration script from the old schema to the new one.
evals.expected_output: Triggers intent-lock. Interview proceeds via option-picker rounds only, uncapped in count but capped at 3 questions per round. Ends only on saturation, LOCK/stop-asking, or the degenerate-loop guard — never on a round-count ceiling.

evals.name: thai-trigger-lock-pao-mai
evals.prompt: ล็อคเป้าหมายก่อนนะ ผมอยากได้ระบบแจ้งเตือนสำหรับแอปคลินิก
evals.expected_output: Triggers on the Thai phrase ล็อคเป้าหมาย. Runs the same silent Phase 0 / picker-round protocol as the English triggers. Response register matches the user's Thai (or Thai-English code-switch) rather than switching to English prose.

evals.name: unprompted-offer-expensive-ambiguous-request
evals.prompt: Build me the full reporting pipeline — pull from all our data sources, clean it, and generate the exec summary however makes sense.
evals.expected_output: No trigger phrase was said. Because the request is expensive (multi-stage pipeline), reusable, and ambiguous ("however makes sense", unspecified sources, unspecified summary shape), the skill offers itself unprompted before building — either by opening the interview directly or naming in one line why alignment is warranted first. Does not silently proceed to build a guessed pipeline.

evals.name: ask-me-until-you-understand-trigger
evals.prompt: Ask me until you understand exactly what I want for this onboarding email sequence — don't guess.
evals.expected_output: Triggers intent-lock. No question is ever asked in prose — every Phase 1 question goes through the option-picker with a mandatory escape option. Never proposes a bigger or better version of the onboarding sequence than what was asked. Never emits a portable prompt/spec artifact for the user to copy elsewhere.

evals.name: negative-precise-one-line-request
evals.prompt: Rename the variable `usrId` to `userId` in src/auth.ts.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT trigger intent-lock. The request already has exactly one reading — cost of a wrong reading is near zero and there is nothing to interrogate. FAILS if an interview, option-picker, or ledger read is initiated for a request this precise and cheap.

evals.name: negative-post-delivery-complaint-routes-to-misread-capture
evals.prompt: You wasted my time — that's not even close to what I asked for.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT trigger intent-lock (no forward-looking build to interrogate). This is a post-delivery complaint about work already shipped, so it routes to misread-capture instead: two questions (what was actually wanted, when the tell arrived), captured in the user's own words, with no self-diagnosis or defense of the prior work. FAILS if intent-lock's interview protocol (picker rounds, cold read, convergence gates) is invoked here.

evals.name: negative-scaffolding-request-plugin-creator-territory
evals.prompt: /new-plugin — I want to scaffold a new plugin for tracking client invoices.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT trigger intent-lock. This is a plugin-scaffolding request that belongs to plugin-creator's own elicitation flow, not a pre-build alignment gate on an arbitrary task. FAILS if intent-lock's option-picker interview protocol is invoked instead of deferring to plugin-creator.
